title: pteridium aquilinum subsp. capense
intro: Pteridium aquilinum subsp. capense, also known as the Cape Brake fern or South African Bracken, is a subspecies of the Pteridium aquilinum fern species. It is a type of flowering plant in the fern family (Pteridaceae) characterized by its large, lance-shaped fronds and distinctive red-brown stipes. The Cape Brake fern has been traditionally used in medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, with medicinal preparations including infusions, decoctions, and salves. The health benefits of Pteridium aquilinum subsp. capense include pain relief, wound healing, and potentially even anti-cancer properties, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy in these areas.
